rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE GISIE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 15, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY and SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING, INC.'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is continued to July 9, 2021 on Court's own motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. LAPRAY BURKE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 15, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT LAPRAY BURKE'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Issues of fact cannot be addressed on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JOHN KOURY VS. MATTHEW GRAHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 15, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT MATTHEW GRAHAM'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to reconcile allegations appearing the complaint paragraph 1 and in the Lease paragraphs 1, 37. If Plaintiff is suing in his capacity as the Trustee of the owner Trust, Plaintiff must so indicate. If Plaintiff is the owner in his individual capacity plaintiff to assert facts re: paragraph 37 of the Lease. Demurrer is otherwise OVERRULED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. MICAH KEMP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 15, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT MICAH KEMP'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations in the Notice and Complaint. Plaintiff's attempt to do so in the First Amended Complaint is not supported by any legal authority, i.e. there is no legal authority cited in opposition that Plaintiff may unilaterally decide that SFRO is applicable to a tenancy for the purposes of "pursuing this action," while it is otherwise inapplicable. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
